sql.help_hubs_node_fk_update:
-- ============================================================
-- help_hubs: set geo_reference_node_id via node key
-- Run after geo_reference_nodes and help_hubs are seeded.
-- ============================================================
UPDATE help_hubs
  SET geo_reference_node_id = (SELECT id FROM geo_reference_nodes WHERE key = 'north-shore-oahu-boil-water-zone')
  WHERE external_id = 'oahu-boil-water-notice-mokuleia-turtle-bay';
UPDATE help_hubs
  SET geo_reference_node_id = (SELECT id FROM geo_reference_nodes WHERE key = 'haleiwa-oahu')
  WHERE external_id = 'oahu-foodbank-north-shore-mobile-distribution';
sql.need_summaries_node_fk_update:
-- ============================================================
-- public_need_summaries: set geo_reference_node_id via node key
-- ============================================================
UPDATE public_need_summaries
  SET geo_reference_node_id = (SELECT id FROM geo_reference_nodes WHERE key = 'north-shore-oahu-boil-water-zone')
  WHERE external_id = 'need-oahu-boil-water-north-shore';
UPDATE public_need_summaries
  SET geo_reference_node_id = (SELECT id FROM geo_reference_nodes WHERE key = 'haleiwa-oahu')
  WHERE external_id = 'need-oahu-north-shore-cleanup-volunteers';
UPDATE public_need_summaries
  SET geo_reference_node_id = (SELECT id FROM geo_reference_nodes WHERE key = 'haleiwa-oahu')
  WHERE external_id = 'need-oahu-food-north-shore-central';
sql.full_migration_sql:
-- ── Schema additions: geo_reference_nodes + FK columns ──────────────────────
-- Add to supabase/schema.sql before first apply.
-- geo_reference_nodes table: see dev-confirmed DDL.
-- FK columns to add to existing table definitions:
--
--   help_hubs:
--     geo_reference_node_id uuid references geo_reference_nodes(id)
--
--   public_need_summaries:
--     geo_reference_node_id uuid references geo_reference_nodes(id)
--
-- Both nullable. help_hubs keeps exact lat/lng for real facilities;
-- geo_reference_node_id used only when a record is area-level or approximate.
-- public_need_summaries uses geo_reference_node_id as primary map anchor
-- (no lat/lng on that table per spec).
--
-- Seeding order:
--   1. geo_reference_nodes (below)
--   2. help_hubs, public_need_summaries (existing seed files)
--   3. FK linkage UPDATE statements (below)

-- ============================================================
-- geo_reference_nodes  (14 records)
-- ============================================================
INSERT INTO geo_reference_nodes (key, name, island, area, latitude, longitude, radius_km, notice_type, notes, is_active) VALUES ('honolulu-oahu', 'Honolulu, Oʻahu', 'Oʻahu', 'Honolulu', 21.3069, -157.8583, NULL, NULL, 'General anchor for Honolulu metro area resources.', true)
  ON CONFLICT (key) DO UPDATE SET name = EXCLUDED.name, island = EXCLUDED.island, area = EXCLUDED.area, latitude = EXCLUDED.latitude, longitude = EXCLUDED.longitude, radius_km = EXCLUDED.radius_km, notice_type = EXCLUDED.notice_type, notes = EXCLUDED.notes, is_active = EXCLUDED.is_active;

UI should label this as Area notice / Approximate area, not a point facility. Update is_active=false when BWS lifts the notice.', true)

-- ============================================================
-- help_hubs: set geo_reference_node_id via node key
-- Run after geo_reference_nodes and help_hubs are seeded.
-- ============================================================
UPDATE help_hubs
  SET geo_reference_node_id = (SELECT id FROM geo_reference_nodes WHERE key = 'north-shore-oahu-boil-water-zone')
  WHERE external_id = 'oahu-boil-water-notice-mokuleia-turtle-bay';
UPDATE help_hubs
  SET geo_reference_node_id = (SELECT id FROM geo_reference_nodes WHERE key = 'haleiwa-oahu')
  WHERE external_id = 'oahu-foodbank-north-shore-mobile-distribution';

-- ============================================================

-- ============================================================
UPDATE public_need_summaries
  SET geo_reference_node_id = (SELECT id FROM geo_reference_nodes WHERE key = 'north-shore-oahu-boil-water-zone')
  WHERE external_id = 'need-oahu-boil-water-north-shore';
UPDATE public_need_summaries
  SET geo_reference_node_id = (SELECT id FROM geo_reference_nodes WHERE key = 'haleiwa-oahu')
  WHERE external_id = 'need-oahu-north-shore-cleanup-volunteers';
UPDATE public_need_summaries
  SET geo_reference_node_id = (SELECT id FROM geo_reference_nodes WHERE key = 'haleiwa-oahu')
  WHERE external_id = 'need-oahu-food-north-shore-central';

geo_reference_nodes.name: North Shore Oʻahu – Boil Water Notice Zone (Mokuleia to Turtle Bay)
geo_reference_nodes.notes: Active boil water notice issued 4:15 p.m. March 21 2026 via HNL Alert. Affects all water users from Mokuleia to Turtle Bay. Coordinate set to approximate corridor midpoint (Dillingham Airfield area). UI should label this as Area notice / Approximate area, not a point facility. Update is_active=false when BWS lifts the notice.

notes: 14 geo_reference_nodes: 13 general anchors, 1 active notice zone (boil water).
Seeding order: (1) geo_reference_nodes, (2) help_hubs + public_need_summaries, (3) FK UPDATE statements. The UPDATEs use key-based subqueries so they are safe to re-run.
Boil water zone (north-shore-oahu-boil-water-zone): notice_type='boil_water', is_active=true. Set is_active=false when BWS lifts the notice — no other records need updating.
2 help_hubs linked to nodes: boil water notice (zone node) and mobile food distribution (Haleʻiwa anchor). All other hubs either have exact coords or are statewide/online-only.

3 public_need_summaries linked to nodes: boil water (zone node), cleanup volunteers (Haleʻiwa), food distribution (Haleʻiwa). Statewide financial assistance need left without node — island field handles display.
radius_km is NULL on all nodes per dev guidance — no overlay rendering yet. Set when the UI is ready to render area overlays.
notice_type observed values: 'boil_water'. Candidates for later: 'evacuation_zone', 'road_closure', 'flood_watch'. Harden into CHECK constraint when the value set stabilizes.
donation_links.geo_reference_node_id deferred per dev recommendation. No current donation records require area-level node anchoring.
All node keys use kebab-case with island suffix for uniqueness. Human-stable — safe to reference in seed scripts across runs.
Unicode island names canonical throughout: Oʻahu, Molokaʻi, Lānaʻi.
